name: Durable Agent XLSX Workbook Builder
description: Create a requested Excel workbook in an xlsx-capable OpenShell sandbox, then upload it to OneDrive and verify access through Microsoft Excel.

document.name: durable-agent-xlsx-sandbox-validation
document.title: Durable Agent XLSX Workbook Builder
document.summary: Provision an OpenShell workspace using the dapr-agent-xlsx sandbox template, install the xlsx skill, create a requested workbook with data, analysis sheets, charts, and a Summary sheet, upload it to OneDrive, and verify it through Microsoft Excel.

input.promptPlaceholder: Example: Search for S&P 500 daily performance numbers, add them to a new Excel file, create useful pivot-style summary tables and charts, and upload it to OneDrive.

maxTurns.label: Max Turns
maxTurns.description: Iteration budget for data collection and workbook creation.

with.prompt:
Use the xlsx skill to create a new Excel workbook that satisfies the request, then leave the completed .xlsx file in the sandbox for the parent workflow to upload to OneDrive.

Workbook request:
{{trigger.prompt}}

Execution rules:
- Invoke the Skill tool with skill name xlsx before creating or editing the workbook.
- Use only the provided workspace tools, WebFetch for public machine-readable data retrieval, and the Skill tool. Do not use OneDrive or Excel tools in this agent run; the parent workflow handles upload and Microsoft Excel verification after this step.
- First run `pwd && python3 --version` and check whether xlsxwriter and openpyxl are importable.
- Do not install packages at runtime. Do not run pip, apt, npm, or package-manager commands.
- Network access is allowed only for retrieving public data needed by the workbook request. Use Python standard-library urllib, already-installed command-line tools, or WebFetch against a direct CSV/JSON URL. Do not use shell network commands for Microsoft Graph, OneDrive, or Excel.
- For any market, finance, or statistical dataset, use only machine-readable CSV or JSON sources. Do not manually construct, infer, or approximate data from search result snippets, web page prose, or JavaScript-rendered tables. If no machine-readable source is reachable, write status=failure in xlsx-local-result.json and explain the source failure.
- If the request asks for S&P 500 daily performance data, use Yahoo Finance chart JSON as the primary no-key source: https://query1.finance.yahoo.com/v8/finance/chart/%5EGSPC?range=1mo&interval=1d. Fetch it with urllib and a browser-like User-Agent when possible; if sandbox egress blocks urllib, use WebFetch on that exact JSON endpoint. Parse the timestamp and close arrays, compute daily point and percent changes from consecutive close values, and document the exact source URL, retrieval timestamp, symbol, range, interval, row count, and calculation notes in the workbook and JSON metadata. If Yahoo is unavailable, try another no-key machine-readable CSV/JSON source; do not fall back to snippets.
- Create /sandbox/validation-output.
- Create /sandbox/validation-output/workbook-output.xlsx. Prefer xlsxwriter for new analytical workbooks because Excel Online reads those files reliably; use openpyxl only when it is a better fit for the request.
- The workbook must include a Summary worksheet. Summary!A1 must contain a concise workbook title, Summary!A2 or nearby cells must summarize the request and data source(s), and Summary must include a visible status row with value success once the workbook is complete.
- For data-analysis requests, include a raw or cleaned Data sheet, at least one analysis sheet with pivot-style summary tables or actual Excel pivot tables if supported by available tooling, and one or more useful charts when the data supports charts.
- Use formulas, tables, number/date formats, frozen headers, and readable sheet names where useful.
- Locally verify the workbook by opening it as a zip file and checking workbook metadata, worksheet XML, shared strings or inline strings, and chart XML entries when charts were created.
- Write /sandbox/validation-output/xlsx-local-result.json with valid JSON containing status, requestDescription, workbookPath, oneDriveFileName, workbookTitle, summarySheetName, dataSources, sheetsCreated, chartCount, analysisNotes, runtimePackageCheck, localZipValidation, and validatedAt. Set workbookPath to /sandbox/validation-output/workbook-output.xlsx and choose a safe .xlsx oneDriveFileName derived from the request plus a timestamp. dataSources must list the machine-readable source URL(s), retrieval timestamp(s), source type, row count, and any calculations performed.
- Print the JSON result and a concise listing of /sandbox/validation-output.
- Do not base64 encode the workbook and do not upload it.
- Do not ask for confirmation.
- End with a concise final answer that says the workbook is ready for upload.
with.stopCondition: Stop after /sandbox/validation-output/workbook-output.xlsx and /sandbox/validation-output/xlsx-local-result.json exist, xlsx-local-result.json has status=success plus oneDriveFileName, workbookTitle, summarySheetName=Summary, and at least one sheet listed in sheetsCreated. Do not upload to OneDrive or call Excel tools from this child agent.

agentConfig.name: durable-agent-xlsx-workbook-builder
agentConfig.instructions: Use the xlsx skill and the xlsx-capable sandbox to create the requested Excel workbook at /sandbox/validation-output/workbook-output.xlsx. Treat this child run as local workbook production only: load the xlsx skill, inspect the Python runtime, fetch public data only when needed by the workbook request, and use only machine-readable CSV or JSON sources for market, finance, or statistical data. Never synthesize numeric datasets from search snippets, web prose, or JavaScript-rendered tables; fail clearly if a machine-readable source cannot be reached. Create a polished workbook with a Summary sheet, data/analysis sheets, pivot-style tables or actual pivots when supported, and charts when useful, validate it locally as an .xlsx zip, write /sandbox/validation-output/xlsx-local-result.json with status=success and upload metadata, then stop. Do not base64 encode, upload, call OneDrive, or call Excel; the parent workflow performs cloud upload and Excel verification in deterministic follow-up tasks.

skills.name: xlsx
skills.description: Create, inspect, transform, and validate Excel .xlsx workbooks.
